title: Mood induction in MDD and healthy adolescents

funding_references.award_title: Intramural Research Program of the National Institute of Mental Health

description: This dataset contains magnetoencephalography (MEG) and structural magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) data from adolescent participants with major depressive disorder and healthy controls during a monetary gambling mood induction task and resting state. The study investigates electrophysiological correlates of mood and reward dynamics in human adolescents through pre-registered analyses of task-based and resting-state MEG recordings paired with anatomical MRI. Note: Data from four subjects are not included due to missing data sharing consent. Two additional subjects (sub-22658 and sub-24247) lack structural MRI scans.
methods_description: MEG data were acquired during a monetary gambling mood induction task (task-mmi3) and a 10-minute resting state scan (task-rest) in the same session. Task-mmi3 data have been preprocessed with identification of bad channels and bad segments (motion >5mm and/or noise artifacts). Anatomical MRI scans were defaced with co-registered fiducial coordinates provided in anatomical JSON files.
